role_title: Principal Program Manager, Tech Risk BC/DR

location.office_time_required: majority of work remote; most teams meet once or twice per quarter for 2-4 consecutive days

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in business continuity, disaster recovery, or related fields
Title 'Principal Program Manager' signals senior IC level
Comp range $172,100–$238,300 base, consistent with Principal IC at a fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Information Security, Computer Science, Business, Risk Management, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience, plus 8 years of experience in business continuity, disaster recovery, operational resilience, information security, risk management, or program management.
8+ years of experience leading or materially maturing business continuity, disaster recovery, or operational resilience programs.
Experience designing or maturing business continuity, disaster recovery, or operational resilience programs in a bank, fintech, lending, or other regulated financial services environment.
Experience conducting business impact analyses, recovery planning, dependency mapping, resilience testing, and remediation tracking.

Experience implementing or operating against business continuity, disaster recovery, or operational resilience frameworks or standards such as ISO 22301, NIST, FFIEC, or equivalent regulatory guidance.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of technology resilience concepts, including cloud infrastructure resilience, service outages, incident response coordination, system dependencies, and vendor recovery planning.
Experience supporting audits, regulatory reviews, or compliance initiatives related to business continuity, disaster recovery, or operational resilience.
Skilled in developing scalable operational processes, program governance models, documentation repositories, and executive-level reporting.
Ability to communicate resilience risks, recovery strategies, and program priorities to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Professional certifications such as CBCP, CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or related business continuity, security, or risk certifications

explicitly_excluded: Candidates based in Quebec, Canada (noted for Canadian roles generally)

scope.surface: Enterprise business continuity and disaster recovery program within the Information Security team at Upstart Bank
scope.business_model: AI lending marketplace, B2B2C (partners with banks and credit unions)
primary_metrics_inferred: Recovery time objective (RTO) / recovery point objective (RPO) adherence
Test completion rates and remediation tracking
core_responsibilities: Lead the enterprise business continuity and disaster recovery program strategy, execution, governance, and ongoing maturity efforts.

Own and mature the business impact analysis process, including critical business process identification, dependency mapping, ownership assignment, risk-rating methodology, and recovery strategy development.
Develop and maintain business continuity plans, disaster recovery plans, crisis management processes, and supporting documentation for critical business and technology functions.
Drive cross-functional execution across Technology, Security, Office Operations, Vendor Management, Enterprise Risk Management, Compliance, and business process owners.
Coordinate disaster recovery exercises, tabletop tests, remediation tracking, and evidence collection to improve organizational preparedness and support audit or regulatory expectations.
Maintain and improve program repositories, workflows, and reporting, including business impact analysis records, recovery plans, testing evidence, program metrics, and remediation status.

compensation.equity_form: annual equity grants that vest quarterly; Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) with discounted stock purchase options

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($172,100–$238,300 base) and competitive for a principal-level remote role
Fully remote-US eligible with no requirement to be in a specific city
Clear program scope and ownership area — inheriting and maturing existing BC/DR foundations
Generous 401(k) match ($2 for $1 up to $15,000 annually) and equity compensation
Role sits in first line of defense with broad executive visibility
Growing team context (Upstart Bank expansion) signals investment in the function
red_flags: Visa sponsorship eligibility is not explicitly stated — unclear for non-US-citizen candidates
8+ years hard requirement is a high bar and marked as a Minimum Qualification, not preferred
Regulated financial services experience (bank/fintech) is a hard requirement, limiting candidates from non-financial industries
Travel cadence not precisely defined — 'once or twice per quarter for 2-4 consecutive days' could mean up to ~16 days/year of travel

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Principal' and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from standard US remote hiring language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not explicitly addressed in the JD; marked as 'no-sponsorship' and 'unclear' respectively based on absence of any sponsorship offer language.
The JD mentions offices in Columbus, Austin, Bay Area, and NYC but the role is listed as fully remote — cities_listed left empty as they pertain to company offices, not this role's requirement.
ISO 22301 and NIST are listed under 'technologies_named' as they are technical standards/frameworks, not traditional technologies — flagged as a classification judgment call.
